title: 2003-era DDR2 memory prices jump up to 60% AI-driven DRAM shortage reaches the oldest standard still in production

title: Quake changed gaming forever 30 years ago today seminal title established online multiplayer culture and made 3D graphics accelerators essential PC components
summary: On this day in 1996, id Software unleashed Quake on the unsuspecting public. The game s influence is difficult to overstate, with its pioneering 3D engine inspiring the first wave of 3D accelerator PC expansion card purchases, the establishment of online multiplayer competitive culture, and much more.

title: ST VL53L9CX direct Time-of-Flight 3D LiDAR supports 5cm to 9m range, 2.3K zones resolution
summary: STMicroelectronics VL53L9CX, or VL53L9 for short, is the company s first direct Time-of-Flight (dToF) 3D LiDAR all-in-one module. It offers a sensing range of 5 centimeters to 9 meters, a wide 72 field of view, a resolution of 2.3K zones, and up to 100 Hz frame rate. ST s ToF sensors have gone a long way since we covered the VL53L0X sensor with a single zone and 2-meter range in 2017. Since then, the company has steadily improved its tiny ToF sensors with up to 64 zones and long range in recent sensors like the VL53L8CP. While the VL53L9CX is still tiny at 12.8 x 6.1 x 4.6 mm, it s in a class of its own, acting like a mini 3D scanner with 2268 zones, and will be used for a range of applications in robotics, industrial automation, smart buildings, AR/VR, and healthcare. title: UTFS is a lightweight, zero-allocation file system for embedded devices
summary: CLI Systems has introduced UTFS ( TFS), a simple, lightweight embedded storage system designed for small MCUs. Unlike traditional file systems like FAT32 or EXT4, or even lighter options like LittleFS and SPIFFS used on boards like the ESP32, UTFS is much simpler. It does not use dynamic memory and avoids complex features like wear leveling, making it easier to use on low-resource devices. Designed for flat, byte-addressable memory like raw EEPROM, CPU flash pages, or external SPI/I C flash, UTFS allows bare-metal firmware to store named data blobs ( files ) back-to-back. Each file is appended with a fixed 24-byte header containing metadata like identifiers, sizes, and application-defined signatures. UTFS specifications and features Footprint Fixed 24-byte header per file; zero heap usage (no malloc) Format TAR-inspired sequential layout; forward-compatible so files can be added later without breaking existing memory layouts Storage Agnostic Requires porting only two functions to interface with [...] The post UTFS is a lightweight, zero-allocation file system for embedded devices appeared first on CNX Software - Embedded Systems News .

title: Best of Sensors: Stathera wins Best MEMS Solution at Sensors Converge
summary: Strathera's DualMode MEMS oscillator generates both kHz and MHz from a single MEMS resonator via fusion bonding tech. It won Best of Sensors in the MEMS Solution category at Best of Sensors May 6 at Sensors Converge.
